requirement_blocks.rules_and_restrictions: To declare a major in CE, you'll need: 1. To have finished at least one full semester at U of M. 2. An overall GPA of 2.0 or better. 3. To have credit (either by transfer or a letter grade) for at least one course in each of these categories: a. Calculus (such as MATH 115, MATH 116, MATH 120, MATH 121, MATH 156). b. Calculus based physics lectures (such as PHYSICS 140 or PHYSICS 160) or chemistry lectures (CHEM 130). c. Engineering courses (such as ENGR 100 or (ENGR 101 or ENGR 151 or EECS 180 or EECS 183)).

Students must complete one of the following chemistry sequences: (CHEM 125, CHEM 126, and CHEM 130) or (CHEM 210 and CHEM 211). Students must complete one of the following: ENGR 101, ENGR 151, EECS 180, or EECS 183. Students must complete one of the following: MATH 115 or MATH 120. Students must complete one of the following: MATH 116 or MATH 121. Students must complete one of the following: MATH 214, MATH 215, MATH 217, MATH 417, or MATH 419.
At least 3 credits of Humanities classes marked HU in the LSA course guide are required. At least 3 credits must be at the 300 level or higher are required.

Nearly any course at the University of Michigan can be taken as a General Elective and this is how you get to a total of 128 credits. You might find that you have AP credits or transfer credits that can be used here.
Students must choose one from EECS 301, MATH 425, or STATS 412. Students must choose one from EECS 496 or ENGR 499-002.
Computer Engineering students must complete 8 credits (2 courses) from the listed options.

Students must complete at least 10 credits from a minimum of 3 courses. One course must be a Major Design Experience course offering. No more than 4 credits of EECS 482 may be used toward ULCE Electives. Technical elective credits can 'flow down' from previous categories. Once 10 credits in Upper Level CE Electives are earned, additional credits can flow down to EECS Electives.

All Computer Engineering students are required to complete at least one Major Design Experience class from this list. EECS 470 satisfies the MDE requirement if completed in Fall 2025 or earlier. EECS 472 satisfies the MDE requirement if completed in Winter 2026 or later. Note that some courses are not offered every semester.

Other EECS courses may be approved on a case-by-case basis. Technical elective credits can 'flow down' from previous categories. Once 3 credits in EECS Electives are earned, additional credits can flow down to Flexible Technical Electives.

Other courses may be acceptable with prior approval of the Chief Program Advisor. The basic standard is that a class you are proposing as an FTE should involve at least as much technical content as the options on this list. A rough guideline is that it should involve the use of college-level mathematics. If little mathematics beyond arithmetic or basic algebra is involved, it is unlikely to be approved. All tutoring and seminar courses are excluded. Up to 4 credit hours of independent study (EECS 399, EECS 499, ENGR 355, ENGR 455 or other technical department's independent study courses) may be used as Flexible Technical Electives. ENGR 455 credits approved to fulfill the Major Design Experience requirement are exempt from this Directed Study Rule, meaning if ENGR 455 is used to satisfy your MDE checkbox, you can use 4 credits of ENGR 455 as well as up to 4 credits of Directed Study coursework toward your Flexible Technical Electives. For AEROSP, BIOLOGY, BIOMEDE, CHE, CEE, Climate, MECHENG, MCDB, NAVARCH, NERS, Physics, Space, IOE: Any 300-level and above courses are generally acceptable, with specific exclusions for some departments. For MATH: Any class at the 300-level or higher (except MATH 310, MATH 327, MATH 333, MATH 385, MATH 389, MATH 399, MATH 422, MATH 429, MATH 431, MATH 485, MATH 486, MATH 489, MATH 497). Students can only receive credit for one linear algebra course. For EECS: Any 300-level or above (except EECS 314, EECS 402, EECS 403, EECS 406, EECS 409, EECS 410 and EECS 495). EECS 281 may only be used toward FTEs if other courses are used to fulfill the Core Electives area - EECS 281 cannot count in two spots. Technical elective credits can 'flow down' from previous categories.
